symptoms.symptom_name: Acute erythema and oedema of the glans

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inflammation of the glans and prepuce is the core feature of balanoposthitis.
Localised erythema alone does not distinguish superficial balanoposthitis from early cellulitis without examining for shaft spread and systemic features.

symptoms.symptom_name: Thick white clumpy subpreputial discharge with malodour

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A thick white curd-like subpreputial discharge is characteristic of candidal infection.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: bacterial balanitis or sexually transmitted infection
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A classic white clumpy discharge favours candida over a purulent or mucopurulent bacterial/STI discharge, though a swab settles it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recent completed course of broad-spectrum antibiotics

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Broad-spectrum antibiotics disrupt commensal flora and predispose to candidal overgrowth — a key precipitant here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inability to retract the foreskin due to swelling (functional phimosis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inflammatory preputial oedema causing functional phimosis is a recognised complication of balanoposthitis.
An unretractable, swollen, painful foreskin cannot be distinguished from an early paraphimosis without examining for a constricting band.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dysuria (stinging on voiding) with preserved ability to void

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Meatal and periurethral inflammation from balanoposthitis produces dysuria.

symptoms.symptom_name: Potassium hydroxide microscopy of subpreputial discharge showing pseudohyphae and budding yeast

investigation_details.investigation_name: KOH wet-mount microscopy of subpreputial specimen
investigation_details.expected_result: Pseudohyphae and budding yeast, confirming Candida
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Aetiology remains presumptive; a swab is needed if the presentation is atypical or fails to resolve.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation of pseudohyphae/budding yeast confirms candidal aetiology — a laboratory/point-of-care step unavailable over telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of a constricting preputial band on examination (excludes paraphimosis)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Only direct genital examination can confirm there is no trapped, retracted foreskin with a constricting band — the sealed in-person finding that de-escalated this case; the AI cannot obtain it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Afebrile with no erythema spreading up the shaft

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No fever and no proximal spread argue against cellulitis or systemic infection at this time.
Ability to void argues against current acute urinary retention but does not itself confirm or refute the aetiology; it is a key safety-net threshold.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Acute inflamed glans plus thick white subpreputial discharge shortly after broad-spectrum antibiotics is the classic candidal cluster.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person genital examination (glans, prepuce, retractability, exclusion of constricting band)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The examination that confirms balanoposthitis and excludes paraphimosis, cellulitis and obstruction — the step the AI cannot perform and must arrange.
Confirms candidal aetiology; performed at the in-person visit.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Subpreputial/urethral swab for MC&S and STI testing
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Reserved for atypical presentations or failure to respond; not required first-line when the candidal picture is classic.
